mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation and increasing federal funds rates indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle aimed at curbing price pressures. This environment typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced economic activity, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive or flat (within 0.5%) in more than 60% of periods where both FEDFUNDS increases and high CPIAUCSL growth conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts during an early-cycle expansion signify increasing consumer confidence and investment, acting as a strong leading indicator for broader economic growth. This positive sentiment and activity drive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows sustained strength in an early-cycle expansion, but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strong manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, reflecting robust demand and corporate profitability. This positive economic momentum drives S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when INDPRO 3-month annualized growth exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer income and spending. This sustained economic activity provides a positive backdrop for corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and SPX 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth and falling unemployment signals a robust and accelerating economic recovery. This environment of increasing output and improving labor market conditions boosts corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY growth > 2% AND UNRATE falls > 0.2pp over 6 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat (within 0.5%) in more than 40% of periods where both strong GDP growth and falling unemployment conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate, particularly during periods of low or falling inflation, signal an overly aggressive monetary policy that risks stifling economic growth. This scenario typically leads to a contraction in economic activity and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:historical periods of rapid rate hikes
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive or flat (within 0.5%) in more than 50% of periods characterized by rapid FEDFUNDS hikes during a disinflationary environment.
